title: Controlled production canary rollout plan
objective: Progressively route production traffic from Firebase to the platform API across all client surfaces with explicit abort criteria and independent per-surface rollback switches.

abort_criteria.description: Any single criterion triggers immediate surface rollback via feature flag. Cross-surface P0 triggers global pause.

thresholds.abort_if: Request submit/status/history failure rate > 5% for any cohort
Any request_status_events row count divergence from expected range
Push delivery failure rate > 10% over 30-min window

rollout_sequence.name: Internal validation (0% user traffic)
actions: Merge PR #8 (w7-01 migration scripts) to master
Apply all Supabase migrations to production via CI (supabase db push)
Run staging_setup.sh --seed against production-equivalent DB; all checks must pass
Run npm run uat with UAT_SETUP_DB=true against staging; 100% pass required
Run migrate_firebase_to_supabase.py --dry-run against production Firebase; validate row counts match expectations
Execute migrate_firebase_to_supabase.py against production (full run); verify W2-03 DB checks pass post-migration
Deploy platform-api to production (no traffic yet); confirm /v1/health returns 200
Run npm run smoke with SMOKE_AUTH_MODE=off against production endpoint
exit_criteria: All W7-01 UAT checks pass on production DB
Migration row counts match Firebase export counts (+/- 0 divergence)
Platform API /v1/health returns 200 for 24h with zero restarts

rollout_sequence.name: Web-admin read-only canary (10% cohort)
rollout_sequence.traffic_strategy: Route 10% of web-admin sessions to platform API auth and domain reads via flag rollout
actions: Enable ff.web_admin.api_auth_v1 at 10% cohort
Enable ff.web_admin.api_domain_v1 at 10% cohort
Monitor error rate and latency continuously against abort criteria
Compare Firebase vs platform API response payload parity for customer/contractor reads
After 24h healthy soak: escalate to 50%, then 100% with 4h soak per step
rollout_sequence.rollback: Set ff.web_admin.api_auth_v1=false and ff.web_admin.api_domain_v1=false; traffic reverts to Firebase immediately

rollout_sequence.name: Portal read canary (10% cohort)
rollout_sequence.traffic_strategy: Route 10% of portal sessions to platform API request list and history reads
actions: Enable ff.portal.api_requests_v1 at 10% cohort
Enable ff.portal.api_history_v1 at 10% cohort
Verify request list payloads match Firebase read parity
Monitor realtime update delivery gap (Firestore vs platform); no regression allowed
After 24h healthy soak: escalate to 50% then 100% with 4h soak per step

rollout_sequence.name: Mobile write canary (10% cohort)
rollout_sequence.traffic_strategy: Route 10% of mobile users to platform API for request creates and status updates
actions: Enable mobile request write flags at 10% cohort for both Android and iOS
Monitor request submission success rate (must remain >= 99.5%)
Verify request_status_events written correctly for all new mobile-created requests
Verify push notifications delivered for status changes
After 48h healthy soak: escalate to 50% then 100% with 12h soak per step
On 100%: enable ff.android.api_media_v1 and ff.ios.api_media_v1 for signed media flow
rollout_sequence.rollback: Disable all mobile write flags; requests revert to Firebase path

rollout_sequence.name: Full cutover and Firebase shadow disable
rollout_sequence.dependency: Phase 4 stable at 100% for >= 48h; all surfaces stable
actions: Enable ff.web_admin.api_activity_v1 (activity summary endpoints)
Enable ff.portal.realtime_channel_v1 (WebSocket/SSE realtime replacement)
Disable Firebase read shadow mode across all surfaces
Confirm Firebase Firestore read traffic drops to near-zero in Firebase console
Confirm platform API handles 100% of production traffic without degradation
Record cutover timestamp for deprecation window start
exit_criteria: All surfaces stable at 100% platform API for >= 72h
All abort criteria metrics within healthy thresholds for >= 72h
W7-03 post-rollout verification report completed and signed off

monitoring_checklist: Alert on /v1/health failure (from w6-02 alerts baseline)
Dashboard: error rate, p95 latency, active request count per endpoint
Per-surface feature flag activation state visible in monitoring
Firebase Firestore read/write counters tracked during parallel operation
Push notification delivery rate tracked during mobile cutover phases
request_status_events count vs requests count monitored for divergence

on_call_requirements.per_phase: Senior engineer on-call for first 4h of each new phase activation
on_call_requirements.soak_period: Async monitoring with automated alert coverage during soak windows
on_call_requirements.escalation: Per W6-03 runbook: blocker > 4h notify lead, > 24h escalate to program lead
